title: noronhia intermedia
intro: Noronhia intermedia, also known as the Madagascar ironwood, is a plant species native to the island of Madagascar. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antimalarial, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Noronhia intermedia include its ability to reduce fever, treat wounds, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Traditional herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used in folk medicine to treat a range of ailments.
